perturbation.original_text: The CELEBRITY will assist in getting a distributorship started through his contacts and fan mail. The CELEBRITY can designate the party placed in the distributorship direct to the Company. This distributorship will be independent of the endorsement terms of the agreement and will remain on going as long as the CELEBRITY maintains the annual membership fee.
perturbation.changed_text: The CELEBRITY will assist in getting a distributorship started through his contacts and fan mail. The CELEBRITY can designate the party placed in the distributorship direct to the Company. This distributorship will be independent of the endorsement terms of the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'and will remain on going as long as the CELEBRITY maintains the annual membership fee', it creates an inconsistency. Section 4(iii) references the distributorship and the annual dues required to maintain the stock options. Now, without the original clause, the duration and maintenance of the distributorship are undefined, contradicting section 4(iii) which assumes its continuous existence if the CELEBRITY pays annual dues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The COMPANY will give stock to the CELEBRITY which will be restricted for one year from issuance and will be as follows: Monthly Sales Volume Stock Options -------------------- ------------- $ 300,000 300,000 shares 600,000 600,000 shares 1,000,000 1,000,000 shares These stock options will remain in place until certificates are issued once volumes are met as long as the CELEBRITY maintains the distributorship by paying the annual dues of $15.00.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the distributorship would continue as long as the CELEBRITY paid the annual membership fee. By removing this clause, the changed text creates a conflict with the contradicted text, which states that stock options remain in place 'as long as the CELEBRITY maintains the distributorship by paying the annual dues'. The changed text no longer specifies the conditions for maintaining the distributorship, thereby contradicting the assumption made in the stock options clause.

perturbation.original_text: TERMS FOR THE CELEBRITY Work with Jack Zufelt to develop a business strategy that can be implemented within 30 days of the signing of this agreement. (i) Identify marketing platform to recruit new distributors (ii) Develop a recruiting packet for new recruits that are duplicable with most tools coming from the corporate website and / or inventory. (iii) CELEBRITY will be available for phone conferences and meeting participating at any level deemed necessary by the COMPANY. (iv) CELEBRITY will be available for conference calls not to exceed five per month and at the discretion of his schedule. (v) The terms of this agreement will remain confidential between CELEBRITY and the COMPANY unless written permission is granted for release by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: TERMS FOR THE CELEBRITY Work with Jack Zufelt to develop a business strategy that can be implemented within 30 days of the signing of this agreement. (i) Identify marketing platform to recruit new distributors (ii) Develop a recruiting packet for new recruits that are duplicable with most tools coming from the corporate website and / or inventory. (iii) CELEBRITY will be available for phone conferences and meeting participating at any level deemed necessary by the COMPANY. (v) The terms of this agreement will remain confidential between CELEBRITY and the COMPANY unless written permission is granted for release by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: Removing clause '(iv) CELEBRITY will be available for conference calls not to exceed five per month and at the discretion of his schedule.' creates a contradiction with Section 2, which states 'The CELEBRITY will be limited to six speaking engagements for the year and five conference calls per month at the company's discretion.' Now there is no clause detailing the terms of the celebrities call availability, which leads to an in-text contradiction when section 2 limits the calls to 5 per month.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The CELEBRITY will be limited to six speaking engagements for the year and five conference calls per month at the company's discretion.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a limit of 'five conference calls per month' in clause (iv). The changed text omits this clause, creating an ambiguity regarding the number of permissible conference calls. The contradicted text states 'The CELEBRITY will be limited to... five conference calls per month'. The omission of the original clause (iv) directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which assumes a defined limit on call frequency, making the contract inconsistent and creating a strong contradiction, as the changed text removes the term of call availability altogether.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the provisions herein, the COMPANY and CELEBRITY agree to indemnify, defend and hold each other harmless from and against all demands, claims, actions, losses, damages, liabilities, costs and expenses, including without limitation, interest, penalties and attorneys' fees and expenses asserted against or imposed or incurred by either party by reason of or resulting from any action or a breach of any representation, warranty, covenant, condition, or agreement of the other party to this Agreement. The CELEBRITY will have full release of liability in regards to product performance and/or law suites resulting from use of the product. This liability will remain the responsibility of the COMPANY and manufacturers.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the provisions herein, the COMPANY and CELEBRITY agree to indemnify, defend and hold each other harmless from and against all demands, claims, actions, losses, damages, liabilities, costs and expenses, including without limitation, interest, penalties and attorneys' fees and expenses asserted against or imposed or incurred by either party by reason of or resulting from any action or a breach of any representation, warranty, covenant, condition, or agreement of the other party to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'The CELEBRITY will have full release of liability in regards to product performance and/or law suites resulting from use of the product. This liability will remain the responsibility of the COMPANY and manufacturers.' it creates an inconsistency regarding liability. The removal of this clause means the contract no longer explicitly releases the celebrity from liability regarding product performance, potentially contradicting the general indemnification clause earlier in the section which aims to hold both parties harmless.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the provisions herein, the COMPANY and CELEBRITY agree to indemnify, defend and hold each other harmless from and against all demands, claims, actions, losses, damages, liabilities, costs and expenses, including without limitation, interest, penalties and attorneys' fees and expenses asserted against or imposed or incurred by either party by reason of or resulting from any action or a breach of any representation, warranty, covenant, condition, or agreement of the other party to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the clause that explicitly releases the CELEBRITY from liability regarding product performance directly contradicts the general indemnification clause that holds both parties harmless. The contradicted text aims to indemnify both parties, while the original text, prior to the change, specifically carves out an exception for the celebrity regarding product performance. Removing this exception creates a direct conflict in terms of liability, as now the celebrity could be held liable under the general indemnification clause where they previously were not. This is an Omission that creates an In-Text Contradiction.
